title: Phyllanthus goniostemon
intro: Phyllanthus goniostemon is a medicinal plant species, commonly known as the "giant greenchan" or "giant greenchan tree", that belongs to the family Phyllanthaceae. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, jaundice, and liver diseases, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Phyllanthus goniostemon include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, Phyllanthus goniostemon is often used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including liver and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Phyllanthus goniostemon is classified under the family Phyllanthaceae. This family is a part of the order Malpighiales in the angiosperm clade of flowering plants. The order Malpighiales is a member of the larger clade of flowering plants known as the eudicots. The eudicots are a subgroup of the larger group of flowering plants known as the angiosperms. Phyllanthaceae is also known to be a member of the larger group known as the Rosidae.
